steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student must correct the misunderstanding that Pangaea formed before Kenorland. A good answer must explicitly establish which supercontinent came first, using the dates from the notes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Forming and breaking apart over hundreds of millions of years, supercontinents are made up of most or all of Earth's continents.": Describes the supercontinent cycle generally; never mentions Kenorland or Pangaea"s order. ✗
  • "Kenorland formed about 2.6 billion years ago, long before Pangaea's formation about 300 million years ago.": Directly corrects the misunderstanding with both dates, making the chronological order unmistakable. ✓
  • "Pangaea and Kenorland formed at different times in Earth's history.": Notes they differ in timing but never says which came first — leaves the misunderstanding uncorrected. ✗
  • "Kenorland and Pangaea were both supercontinents, single landmasses made up of most or all of the Earth's continents.": Defines supercontinents; says nothing about when each formed. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
